title: CIL Geology — Advanced Mock 12 (Conceptual)
pattern: CIL MT — 200 MCQ · 2 papers · 1 mark each · no negative marking

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: As per Income Tax Department data (June 2026), India's net direct tax collections in early FY27 rose by about what percentage year-on-year?
questions.solution: Net direct tax collections rose ~14.6% YoY to about Rs 5.21 trillion.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: In 2026, which country became the first Central Asian member of the New Development Bank (NDB)?
questions.solution: Uzbekistan became the first Central Asian nation to join the BRICS-backed NDB.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: Which India-born theoretical physicist won the 2025 Wolf Prize in Physics?
questions.solution: Rajasthan-born physicist Jainendra K. Jain received the 2025 Wolf Prize in Physics.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: Which institution ranked first in the QS World University Rankings 2027 (released June 2026)?
questions.solution: Massachusetts Institute of Technology (MIT) ranked first in QS World University Rankings 2027.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: In June 2026, which Indian jurist was elected a Judge of the International Tribunal for the Law of the Sea (ITLOS)?
questions.solution: Indian jurist Bimal N. Patel was elected to ITLOS in June 2026.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: In 2026, PM Narendra Modi received the highest national award of which country?
questions.solution: PM Modi was conferred Slovakia's highest national award in 2026.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: Exercise KHAAN QUEST 2026, in which an Indian Army contingent participated, was held in which country?
questions.solution: The multinational Exercise KHAAN QUEST 2026 was held in Mongolia.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: Who was named the new Chairman of GIC Re in June 2026?
questions.solution: The government named Hitesh Joshi as the new Chairman of GIC Re.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: In 2026, ONGC was assigned to develop a new strategic petroleum reserve (SPR) facility at which location?
questions.solution: ONGC was tasked with building a new underground crude oil SPR cavern at Mangaluru, Karnataka.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: The India–UK CETA and Social Security Agreement were reported to take effect from which date?
questions.solution: Reports indicated the India–UK CETA would take effect from July 15, 2026.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: What is the currency of the United Kingdom?
questions.solution: The UK's currency is the Pound Sterling (£).

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: Where are the headquarters of the United Nations located?
questions.solution: The UN headquarters is located in New York City, USA.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: Where is the headquarters of the World Health Organization (WHO)?
questions.solution: The WHO is headquartered in Geneva, Switzerland.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: Which is India's national space agency?
questions.solution: The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) is India's space agency.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: The Taj Mahal is located in which city?
questions.solution: The Taj Mahal is in Agra, Uttar Pradesh, on the bank of the Yamuna.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: The Sun Temple at Konark is located in which state?
questions.solution: The Konark Sun Temple is in Odisha, a UNESCO World Heritage Site.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: In which year did the Dandi (Salt) March take place?
questions.solution: Gandhi led the Dandi Salt March in 1930 during the Civil Disobedience Movement.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: The Quit India Movement was launched in which year?
questions.solution: The Quit India Movement began in August 1942.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: Who founded the Maurya Empire?
questions.solution: Chandragupta Maurya founded the Maurya Empire around 321 BCE.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: How many players are there in a cricket team on the field?
questions.solution: A cricket team fields eleven players.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: The Thomas Cup is associated with which sport?
questions.solution: The Thomas Cup is the premier men's international team badminton event.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: The Bharat Ratna is India's highest:
questions.solution: The Bharat Ratna is the highest civilian award of India.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: Which institution regulates monetary policy in India?
questions.solution: The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) is the country's central bank and monetary authority.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: GST in India was implemented from which date?
questions.solution: The Goods and Services Tax came into force on 1 July 2017.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: The speed of light in vacuum is approximately:
questions.solution: Light travels at about 3 × 10^8 metres per second in vacuum.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: A vessel holds 80 L of milk. 10 L is drawn out and replaced with water; this is repeated 3 times. The milk remaining is about:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Each draw-and-replace keeps the fraction $(1-x/V)$ of milk; after n repeats this fraction is raised to the n-th power.
**Formula.** $M=V\left(1-\tfrac{x}{V}\right)^{n}$
**Given.** V = 80 L, x = 10 L, n = 3.
**Working.** $=80\left(1-\tfrac{10}{80}\right)^{3}$.
**Answer.** $M\approx 53.59$ L.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: Two trains 140 m and 100 m long move in opposite directions at 72 km/h and 45 km/h. The time to cross each other is:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Opposite directions add speeds; convert km/h to m/s with the factor 5/18, then divide the combined length by the relative speed.
**Formula.** $t=\tfrac{L_1+L_2}{(s_1+s_2)\times\frac{5}{18}}$
**Given.** L₁ = 140 m, L₂ = 100 m, s₁ = 72 km/h, s₂ = 45 km/h.
**Working.** Relative $=(72+45)\times\tfrac{5}{18}=32.5$ m/s; $t=\tfrac{140+100}{32.5}$.
**Answer.** $t=7.38$ s.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: A trader marks goods 40% above cost, then gives a 10% discount. His net profit percentage is:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Net selling factor is the markup factor times the discount factor; profit% is that factor reduced by 1.
**Formula.** $f=\left(1+\tfrac{m}{100}\right)\left(1-\tfrac{d}{100}\right)$
**Given.** Markup m = 40%, discount d = 10%.
**Working.** $f=\left(1+\tfrac{40}{100}\right)\left(1-\tfrac{10}{100}\right)=1.26$.
**Answer.** Profit $=(f-1)\times100=26\%$.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: The angle between the hour and minute hands of a clock at 8:20 is:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** The hour hand sweeps 30° per hour and 0.5° per minute, the minute hand 6° per minute; take the smaller of the two angles.
**Formula.** $\theta=\left|30H-5.5M\right|$
**Given.** H = 8, M = 20.
**Working.** $=\left|30\times8-5.5\times20\right|=130^\circ$; smaller $=\min(130,360-130)$.
**Answer.** $\theta=130^\circ$.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: A pipe fills a tank in 8 h, but with a leak it takes 10 h. The leak alone can empty the full tank in:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** The leak's rate is the fill-rate without the leak minus the slower fill-rate with the leak.
**Formula.** $T=\tfrac{ab}{b-a}$
**Given.** Fill without leak a = 8 h; with leak b = 10 h.
**Working.** Leak rate $=\tfrac1{8}-\tfrac1{10}$; emptying time $=\tfrac{8\times10}{10-8}$.
**Answer.** $T=40$ h.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: A invests Rs 4000 for 6 months and B invests Rs 8000 for 10 months. Out of a total profit of Rs 4500, A's share is:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Profit is split in the ratio of capital × time invested by each partner.
**Formula.** $\text{share}_A=P\cdot\tfrac{x\,m_A}{x\,m_A+y\,m_B}$
**Given.** A: Rs 4000×6 mo; B: Rs 8000×10 mo; profit Rs 4500.
**Working.** Ratio $=24000:80000$; share$_A=4500\times\tfrac{24000}{104000}$.
**Answer.** A's share $=$ Rs 1038.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: The difference between compound and simple interest on Rs 5000 at 10% per annum for 2 years is:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Over 2 years the gap between compound and simple interest is the first year's interest-on-interest.
**Formula.** $CI-SI=P\left(\tfrac{r}{100}\right)^2$
**Given.** P = Rs 5000, r = 10% p.a., t = 2 yr.
**Working.** $=5000\left(\tfrac{10}{100}\right)^2$.
**Answer.** $CI-SI=$ Rs 50.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: A boat covers 135 km downstream in 9 h and the same 135 km upstream in 15 h. Its speed in still water is:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Downstream speed adds the stream; upstream subtracts it. Still-water speed is the average of the two.
**Formula.** $u=\tfrac12\left[(b+s)+(b-s)\right]$
**Given.** Downstream 135 km in 9 h; upstream 135 km in 15 h.
**Working.** Downstream $=\tfrac{135}{9}=15$ km/h, upstream $=\tfrac{135}{15}=9$ km/h.
**Answer.** Still-water speed $u=\tfrac{15+9}{2}=12$ km/h.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: A vessel holds 50 L of milk. 10 L is drawn out and replaced with water; this is repeated 3 times. The milk remaining is about:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Each draw-and-replace keeps the fraction $(1-x/V)$ of milk; after n repeats this fraction is raised to the n-th power.
**Formula.** $M=V\left(1-\tfrac{x}{V}\right)^{n}$
**Given.** V = 50 L, x = 10 L, n = 3.
**Working.** $=50\left(1-\tfrac{10}{50}\right)^{3}$.
**Answer.** $M\approx 25.6$ L.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: A car covers a distance at 45 km/h in 4 hours. The distance is:
questions.solution: Distance = speed × time = 45 × 4 = 180 km.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: A sum doubles itself in 8 years under simple interest. The annual rate is:
questions.solution: To double, SI = P, so rate = 100/T = 100/8 = 12.5%.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: What is the average of the first 20 natural numbers?
questions.solution: Average of first n naturals = (n+1)/2 = (20+1)/2 = 10.5.

questions.stem: Compound interest on Rs 8000 at 12% per annum for 2 years is:

questions.stem: If 20% of a number is 60, the number is:

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: What is 35% of 240?

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: Simple interest on Rs 5000 at 8% per annum for 3 years is:

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: A 200 m long train crosses a pole in 18 seconds. Its speed is:

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: An article sold at Rs 450 incurs a 25% loss. Its cost price is:

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: A can do a work in 18 days and B in 24 days. Working together they finish it in:

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: The LCM of 14 and 21 is:
questions.solution: The lowest common multiple of 14 and 21 is 42.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: The average age of 4 persons is 28 years. If a person aged 36 leaves, the average of the remaining 3 is:
questions.solution: Total = 112; remaining = 112 − 36 = 76; average = 25.33.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: 4 kg of rice at Rs 40/kg is mixed with 3 kg at Rs 50/kg. The average price per kg is:

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: A number divided by 7 gives quotient 14 and remainder 6. The number is:
questions.solution: Number = divisor × quotient + remainder = 7×14+6 = 104.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: The square root of 3025 is:

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: The probability of getting a multiple of 3 on a single throw of a fair die is:
questions.solution: Count the favourable outcomes out of 6 equally likely faces to get 1/3.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: A man walks 5 km towards north, then turns east and walks 12 km. How far is he from the starting point?
questions.solution: Straight-line distance = √(5² + 12²) = √169 = 13 km.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: If A = 1, B = 2, … , Z = 26, what is the sum of the letter values of 'BID'?
questions.solution: Add the positional values of B, I, D to get 15.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Find the next term in the series: 5, 11, 23, 47, 95, ?
questions.solution: Each term = previous × 2 + 1, so the next term = 95 × 2 + 1 = 191.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Pointing to a man, a woman said, 'His mother is the only daughter of my mother.' How is the woman related to the man?
questions.solution: Tracing each relationship step by step gives: Mother.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Statements: Some cats are dogs. All dogs are animals. Which conclusion necessarily follows?
questions.solution: Applying the rules of categorical syllogism, the valid conclusion is: Some cats are animals.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: In a certain code, each letter of a word is moved two steps forward in the English alphabet. The code for 'FISH' is:
questions.solution: Replace every letter by the one two places ahead: FISH → HKUJ.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: A man walks 6 km towards north, then turns east and walks 8 km. How far is he from the starting point?
questions.solution: Straight-line distance = √(6² + 8²) = √100 = 10 km.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: If A = 1, B = 2, … , Z = 26, what is the sum of the letter values of 'ACE'?
questions.solution: Add the positional values of A, C, E to get 9.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Which of these fractions is the largest?
questions.solution: Comparing decimal values, 4/5 is the largest.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: If A=1, B=2, ... then the sum of the letters of BED is:
questions.solution: Add the alphabet positions of B, E, D to get 11.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Hand : Glove :: Foot : ?
questions.solution: Just as Glove relates to Hand, Sock relates to Foot.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: A man walks 6 km north, then 8 km east. His distance from the start is:
questions.solution: Resultant = √(6² + 8²) = 10 km.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Find the next term: 4, 9, 16, 25, ?
questions.solution: These are perfect squares; next = 6² = 36.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Find the next term: GH, JK, MN, ?
questions.solution: Each pair skips one letter; after MN comes PQ.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: If each letter is replaced by the next letter, ROSE is coded as:
questions.solution: Shift each letter forward by one to get SPTF.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Find the next term: 7, 21, 63, 189, ?
questions.solution: Each term is multiplied by 3, so next = 189 × 3 = 567.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Which is the odd one out: 3, 5, 9, 11?
questions.solution: 3, 5, 11 are prime; 9 is composite.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Find the next term: M, N, O, P, ?
questions.solution: Consecutive alphabet letters; after P comes Q.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: A person facing south turns 90° to the left. Now they face:
questions.solution: Turning left from south leads to East.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Find the next term: 3, 5, 9, 15, ?
questions.solution: Differences grow by 2; adding the next difference gives 23.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Which is the odd one out: Apple, Mango, Banana, Potato?
questions.solution: Potato belongs to a different category from the rest.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Which is the odd one out: 1, 8, 20, 27?
questions.solution: The others are perfect cubes; 20 is not.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Find the next term: Z, X, V, T, ?
questions.solution: Letters decrease by 2 each time, so the next letter is R.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.solution: Each number is halved, so 16 → 8.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: The angle between the hands of a clock at 5:00 is:
questions.solution: Each hour = 30°; at 5:00 the hands are 5 × 30 = 150° apart.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Choose the correct meaning of the word 'Frugal'.
questions.solution: 'Frugal' means careful with money, i.e. economical.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Choose the synonym of 'Abundant'.
questions.solution: 'Abundant' means existing in large quantity, i.e. plentiful.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Choose the synonym of 'Brief'.
questions.solution: 'Brief' means short or concise.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Choose the synonym of 'Happy'.
questions.solution: 'Happy' is synonymous with 'joyful'.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Choose the synonym of 'Enormous'.
questions.solution: 'Enormous' means very large, i.e. huge.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Choose the synonym of 'Diligent'.
questions.solution: 'Diligent' describes someone hard-working and careful.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Choose the synonym of 'Candid'.
questions.solution: 'Candid' means open and frank in speech.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Choose the synonym of 'Vivid'.
questions.solution: 'Vivid' means bright, clear and intense.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Choose the antonym of 'Ancient'.
questions.solution: 'Ancient' (very old) is opposite to 'modern'.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Choose the antonym of 'Victory'.
questions.solution: 'Victory' is opposite to 'defeat'.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Choose the antonym of 'Expand'.
questions.solution: 'Expand' is opposite to 'contract'.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Choose the antonym of 'Generous'.
questions.solution: 'Generous' is opposite to 'stingy' (mean with money).

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Choose the antonym of 'Transparent'.
questions.solution: 'Transparent' (see-through) is opposite to 'opaque'.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Choose the antonym of 'Optimist'.
questions.solution: An optimist's opposite is a 'pessimist'.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Choose the correctly spelt word.
questions.solution: The correct spelling is 'Occasion' (one c, two s).

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Fill in the blank: 'She is good ___ mathematics.'
questions.solution: The idiom is 'good at' a subject or skill.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Fill in the blank: 'He has been living here ___ 2010.'
questions.solution: 'Since' is used with a point of time like a year.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Fill in the blank: 'Divide this ___ two parts.'
questions.solution: 'Divide into' is the correct collocation for separation.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Fill in the blank: 'He is married ___ a doctor.'
questions.solution: The correct collocation is 'married to' someone.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Fill in the blank: 'The book is ___ the table.'
questions.solution: 'On' indicates resting on a surface.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: One word for 'a person who cannot read or write':
questions.solution: Such a person is 'illiterate'.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: The study of living organisms is called:
questions.solution: 'Biology' is the study of all living organisms.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: One who studies stars and celestial bodies is an:
questions.solution: An 'astronomer' scientifically studies celestial bodies.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: One word for 'a speech made without preparation':
questions.solution: An unprepared speech is delivered 'extempore'.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: A place where birds are kept is called:
questions.solution: An 'aviary' houses birds; an apiary keeps bees.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Which statement about coalification is correct?
options: It increases fixed carbon and reduces volatile matter
questions.solution: Progressive burial heat raises rank: more fixed carbon, less moisture and volatiles.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: As coal rank increases from lignite to anthracite, the volatile matter and moisture generally:
questions.solution: Higher rank means more fixed carbon and less moisture/volatiles, raising calorific value.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: On the block diagram shown, the inclined bedding plane dips at 60°. The dip direction is, by definition:
options: Perpendicular to the strike, in the direction of steepest descent
questions.solution: Dip (here 60°) is measured perpendicular to strike, down the line of steepest slope of the plane.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The chemical composition of quartz is:
questions.solution: Quartz is silicon dioxide, SiO₂, one of the commonest minerals.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A bed drops 30 m over a horizontal distance of 20 m. Its dip angle is about:
questions.solution: dip = tan⁻¹(rise/run) = tan⁻¹(30/20) ≈ 56.3°.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Most of India's coal occurs in rocks of which geological system?
questions.solution: The bulk of Indian coal lies in Lower Gondwana (Permian) sediments, e.g. the Barakar Formation.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Igneous rocks are formed by the:
options: Cooling and solidification of magma or lava
questions.solution: Igneous rocks crystallize from molten material (magma below ground, lava above).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The section shows 20 m of overburden above a 3 m coal seam. The volumetric stripping ratio (overburden : coal thickness) is:
questions.solution: Stripping ratio = overburden/coal = 20/3 = 6.67 : 1.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Gravity and magnetic surveys are examples of ____ geophysical methods:
questions.solution: Gravity and magnetics map potential fields arising from density and susceptibility contrasts.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A bed drops 30 m over a horizontal distance of 40 m. Its dip angle is about:
questions.solution: dip = tan⁻¹(rise/run) = tan⁻¹(30/40) ≈ 36.9°.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The coal rank with the highest fixed-carbon content and calorific value is:
questions.solution: Anthracite is the highest-rank coal, with the greatest carbon content and heating value.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Limestone is composed predominantly of the mineral:
questions.solution: Limestone is a carbonate rock chiefly of calcite.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A mineral specimen of mass 105 g has a volume of 30 cm³. Its specific gravity is about:
questions.solution: SG = mass/volume = 105/30 = 3.5.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A mineral's tendency to break along smooth planes related to its atomic structure is its:
questions.solution: Cleavage reflects planes of weak bonding in the crystal lattice.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A dipping coal seam outcrops over a horizontal width of 80 m on flat ground and dips at 35°. Its true thickness is about:
questions.solution: True thickness = horizontal width × sin(dip) = 80×sin35° ≈ 45.89 m.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Heavy minerals concentrated by flowing water in stream beds form a:
questions.solution: Placers (e.g. of gold, cassiterite) form where moving water sorts dense, durable grains.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The magnitude of an earthquake is commonly expressed on the:
questions.solution: Earthquake size is reported as magnitude (Richter / moment magnitude).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The fold shown in cross-section (beds sag downward about the axial plane) is a(n):
questions.solution: Downward-sagging limbs dipping toward the axis define a syncline (youngest beds in core).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Foliation (planar fabric) is a feature of which rock type?
questions.solution: Directed pressure aligns minerals into foliation in metamorphic rocks like schist and gneiss.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A P-wave travels at 8 km/s and arrives 30 s after the earthquake. The (approx.) distance to the source is:
questions.solution: distance = velocity × time = 8×30 = 240 km.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Which property makes a fossil a good index fossil?
options: Short geological range plus wide geographic distribution
questions.solution: Brief existence pins a narrow time; wide spread allows correlation across regions.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Bowen's reaction series predicts that the first silicate mineral to crystallize from a cooling basaltic magma is:
questions.solution: In the discontinuous series olivine crystallizes first at the highest temperature; quartz last.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A rock retains 50% of its original parent isotope (half-life 0.7 Gyr). Its radiometric age is about:
questions.solution: 50% remaining ⇒ 1 half-lives ⇒ age = 1×0.7 = 0.7 Gyr.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The Earth's outer core is inferred to be liquid because:
options: Both P- and S-waves speed up there
questions.solution: Shear (S) waves cannot travel through liquids, creating an S-wave shadow zone — evidence of a liquid outer core.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An aquifer has hydraulic conductivity 10 m/day and a hydraulic gradient of 0.05. The Darcy velocity is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A buried erosion surface separating older from much younger rocks is a(n):
questions.solution: An unconformity represents a gap (missing time) in the rock record.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The law of superposition states that, in an undisturbed sequence, the oldest layer is:
questions.solution: Sediments are deposited oldest-first, so the lowest undisturbed bed is the oldest.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An aquifer has hydraulic conductivity 20 m/day and a hydraulic gradient of 0.02. The Darcy velocity is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: We currently live in which geological era?
questions.solution: The Cenozoic ('age of mammals') is the present era, following the Mesozoic.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: In the faulted block shown, the hanging wall has moved up over the footwall. This is a:
questions.solution: Hanging wall up = reverse fault, caused by compression.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 260 130" width="260" height="130"><line x1="40" y1="25" x2="150" y2="80" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="2"/><rect x="20" y="40" width="120" height="70" fill="#fde68a" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1" transform="skewX(-10)"/><rect x="150" y="40" width="100" height="70" fill="#bfdbfe" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1"/><line x1="60" y1="55" x2="135" y2="92" stroke="#1e293b" stroke-width="1.5" stroke-dasharray="2 2"/><line x1="160" y1="60" x2="245" y2="60" stroke="#1e293b" stroke-width="1.5" stroke-dasharray="2 2"/><text x="190" y="52" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">hanging wall</text><text x="40" y="35" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">footwall</text></svg>
figure.caption: reverse fault

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Mica is well known for its perfect:
options: Basal cleavage (it splits into thin sheets)
questions.solution: Mica has one perfect cleavage direction, peeling into thin elastic sheets.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Which statement about the Moho is correct?
options: It separates the outer and inner core
It separates the crust from the mantle and shows a velocity increase
Seismic velocity drops sharply across it
questions.solution: The Mohorovičić discontinuity marks the crust–mantle boundary, where P-wave velocity jumps.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An ore body of 2 Mt has an average metal grade of 3%. The contained metal is about:
questions.solution: Metal = tonnage × grade = 2 Mt × 3% = 60 thousand tonnes (kt).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Gneiss is characterised by:
options: Banding (segregation of light and dark minerals)
questions.solution: Gneiss is a high-grade foliated rock showing compositional banding (gneissic foliation).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A mineral has passed through 3 half-lives of an isotope whose half-life is 5.73 (in the given time units). Its age is:
questions.solution: Age = number of half-lives × half-life = 3×5.73 = 17.19.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Walther's law states that facies occurring in a conformable vertical succession also occur:
options: Laterally adjacent in the original depositional environments
questions.solution: Conformably superposed facies represent environments that were originally side by side and migrated.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Coalbed methane (CBM) is held in coal seams chiefly by:
options: Free gas in large open caverns
Adsorption onto the coal's internal surfaces
questions.solution: CBM is mostly adsorbed on micropore surfaces; lowering pressure (dewatering) desorbs and releases it.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A coal sample (proximate analysis) has 5% moisture, 30% volatile matter and 20% ash. Its fixed carbon is:
questions.solution: Fixed C = 100 − (moisture + VM + ash) = 100 − (5+30+20) = 45%.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: In an ore, the valueless minerals associated with the ore mineral are called:
questions.solution: Gangue is the commercially worthless material that must be separated from the ore mineral.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A rock sample of 100 cm³ contains 20 cm³ of pore space. Its porosity is:
questions.solution: Porosity = (void volume/total volume)×100 = 20/100 = 20%.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Arranged from lowest to highest rank, the coal series is:
questions.solution: Increasing rank (carbon content) goes peat, lignite, sub-bituminous/bituminous, anthracite.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Darcy's law states that groundwater discharge is proportional to the hydraulic gradient and the:
questions.solution: Q = K·i·A; flow rises with hydraulic conductivity K and gradient i.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: On the block diagram shown, the inclined bedding plane dips at 45°. The dip direction is, by definition:
options: Perpendicular to the strike, in the direction of steepest descent
questions.solution: Dip (here 45°) is measured perpendicular to strike, down the line of steepest slope of the plane.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 240 130" width="240" height="130"><polygon points="20,40 200,40 220,70 40,70" fill="#e2e8f0" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.5"/><line x1="40" y1="70" x2="220" y2="70" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="2"/><text x="120" y="86" font-size="9" fill="#2563eb">strike</text><line x1="120" y1="70" x2="160" y2="110" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="2"/><text x="150" y="100" font-size="10" fill="#dc2626">dip 45°</text><path d="M120 70 A 30 30 0 0 1 150 92" fill="none" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="1"/></svg>
figure.caption: Strike and dip of a bed

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Sedimentary rocks are formed mainly by:
questions.solution: Weathered material is transported, deposited, compacted and cemented into sedimentary rock.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A bed dipping at 60° shows a width of 20 m across a horizontal surface. Its true (perpendicular) thickness is about:
questions.solution: True thickness = width × sin(dip) = 20×sin60° ≈ 17.32 m.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: In a plunging anticline eroded to a level surface, the outcrop pattern of beds forms a:
options: V or nose closing in the direction of plunge
questions.solution: Plunging folds produce V-shaped (zig-zag) outcrops; an anticline's nose points in the plunge direction.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A mineral that effervesces (fizzes) when dilute hydrochloric acid is applied is:
questions.solution: Calcite (CaCO₃) reacts with dilute HCl, releasing CO₂ — a standard field test.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: After 3 half-lives, the percentage of the original radioactive parent isotope remaining is:
questions.solution: Fraction remaining = (1/2)^3 = 12.5%.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The boundary separating the crust from the mantle is the:
questions.solution: The Moho marks the crust–mantle boundary, identified by a jump in seismic velocity.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The section shows 30 m of overburden above a 4 m coal seam. The volumetric stripping ratio (overburden : coal thickness) is:
questions.solution: Stripping ratio = overburden/coal = 30/4 = 7.5 : 1.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 260 140" width="260" height="140"><rect x="20" y="20" width="220" height="60" fill="#fef3c7" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1"/><text x="130" y="54" font-size="10" text-anchor="middle" fill="#92400e">overburden 30 m</text><rect x="20" y="80" width="220" height="20" fill="#1e293b"/><text x="130" y="94" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#fff">coal seam 4 m</text><rect x="20" y="100" width="220" height="30" fill="#d1d5db" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1"/><text x="130" y="120" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#374151">floor</text></svg>
figure.caption: Coal seam with overburden

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Metamorphic rocks form by the action of:
options: Heat and/or pressure on pre-existing rocks
questions.solution: Metamorphism alters mineralogy and texture in the solid state under heat/pressure.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A good index (guide) fossil should be:
options: Widespread but short-lived in geologic time
questions.solution: Wide distribution plus a short time range makes a fossil useful for correlation/dating.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The section shows 40 m of overburden above a 5 m coal seam. The volumetric stripping ratio (overburden : coal thickness) is:
questions.solution: Stripping ratio = overburden/coal = 40/5 = 8 : 1.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 260 140" width="260" height="140"><rect x="20" y="20" width="220" height="60" fill="#fef3c7" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1"/><text x="130" y="54" font-size="10" text-anchor="middle" fill="#92400e">overburden 40 m</text><rect x="20" y="80" width="220" height="20" fill="#1e293b"/><text x="130" y="94" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#fff">coal seam 5 m</text><rect x="20" y="100" width="220" height="30" fill="#d1d5db" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1"/><text x="130" y="120" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#374151">floor</text></svg>
figure.caption: Coal seam with overburden

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The in-place breakdown of rock by mechanical and chemical processes is:
questions.solution: Weathering disintegrates/decomposes rock in situ; erosion then removes the products.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: In the undisturbed stratigraphic column shown (top to base: A, B, C, D), the oldest layer is:
questions.solution: By the law of superposition, the lowest bed (D) is the oldest in an undisturbed sequence.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 180 150" width="180" height="150"><rect x="40" y="15" width="100" height="28" fill="#fde68a" stroke="#475569"/><text x="90" y="33" font-size="11" text-anchor="middle">A</text><rect x="40" y="43" width="100" height="28" fill="#bfdbfe" stroke="#475569"/><text x="90" y="61" font-size="11" text-anchor="middle">B</text><rect x="40" y="71" width="100" height="28" fill="#bbf7d0" stroke="#475569"/><text x="90" y="89" font-size="11" text-anchor="middle">C</text><rect x="40" y="99" width="100" height="28" fill="#fecaca" stroke="#475569"/><text x="90" y="117" font-size="11" text-anchor="middle">D</text><text x="150" y="33" font-size="9" fill="#475569">top</text><text x="150" y="117" font-size="9" fill="#475569">base</text></svg>
figure.caption: Stratigraphic column

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: On the Mohs scale of hardness, the hardest mineral (value 10) is:
questions.solution: Mohs scale runs from talc (1) to diamond (10), the hardest natural mineral.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: From the surface inward, the major layers of the Earth are:
questions.solution: The Earth is layered into a thin crust, a thick mantle, and a metallic core.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A P-wave travels at 7 km/s and arrives 20 s after the earthquake. The (approx.) distance to the source is:
questions.solution: distance = velocity × time = 7×20 = 140 km.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Slate is the low-grade metamorphic product of:
questions.solution: Fine-grained shale metamorphoses to slate, with characteristic slaty cleavage.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The fold shown in cross-section (beds arch upward about the axial plane) is a(n):
questions.solution: Upward-arching limbs dipping away from the axis define an anticline (oldest beds in core).

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 260 130" width="260" height="130"><path d="M20 90 Q 130 20 240 90" fill="none" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="2"/><path d="M20 110 Q 130 45 240 110" fill="none" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="2"/><line x1="130" y1="15" x2="130" y2="120" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="1" stroke-dasharray="3 2"/><text x="130" y="128" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#dc2626">axial plane</text></svg>
figure.caption: anticline (cross-section)

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Which statement about ore and gangue is correct?
options: Gangue is the valuable metal-bearing mineral
Gangue is the worthless mineral matter associated with the ore
questions.solution: The ore mineral carries the metal of value; the surrounding gangue is discarded or processed away.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A volcanic rock containing many gas-bubble cavities is described as:
questions.solution: Vesicles are voids left by escaping gas in rapidly cooled lava (e.g. scoria, pumice).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An ore body of 1 Mt has an average metal grade of 3%. The contained metal is about:
questions.solution: Metal = tonnage × grade = 1 Mt × 3% = 30 thousand tonnes (kt).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Which statement about coal ranks is INCORRECT?
options: Lignite has a higher carbon content than anthracite
questions.solution: Anthracite, not lignite, has the highest carbon content — the statement is reversed.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: At a station the S-wave arrives 10 s after the P-wave. Using ~8 km per second of S–P delay, the epicentral distance is about:
questions.solution: distance ≈ (S−P time) × 8 km/s = 10×8 = 80 km.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: In the proximate analysis of coal, fixed carbon is obtained by subtracting from 100% the sum of:
questions.solution: Fixed carbon (%) = 100 − (moisture + volatile matter + ash) in proximate analysis.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Which statement about mining method selection is correct?
options: A high stripping ratio favours opencast mining
A high stripping ratio favours underground over opencast mining
questions.solution: When too much overburden must be removed per tonne (high stripping ratio), underground mining is preferred.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: To extract 10 m³ of coal, 45 m³ of overburden must be removed. The stripping ratio is:
questions.solution: Stripping ratio = overburden/coal = 45/10 = 4.5 : 1.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Marble is the metamorphic product of:
questions.solution: Limestone recrystallizes into marble under metamorphism.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The section shows 20 m of overburden above a 5 m coal seam. The volumetric stripping ratio (overburden : coal thickness) is:
questions.solution: Stripping ratio = overburden/coal = 20/5 = 4 : 1.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 260 140" width="260" height="140"><rect x="20" y="20" width="220" height="60" fill="#fef3c7" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1"/><text x="130" y="54" font-size="10" text-anchor="middle" fill="#92400e">overburden 20 m</text><rect x="20" y="80" width="220" height="20" fill="#1e293b"/><text x="130" y="94" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#fff">coal seam 5 m</text><rect x="20" y="100" width="220" height="30" fill="#d1d5db" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1"/><text x="130" y="120" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#374151">floor</text></svg>
figure.caption: Coal seam with overburden

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A rock body that can store and transmit usable amounts of groundwater is a(n):
questions.solution: An aquifer is porous and permeable enough to yield water to wells.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Compared with surface (opencast) mining, underground coal mining is generally chosen when the seam is:
options: Deep, so that the stripping ratio becomes uneconomic
questions.solution: When overburden-to-coal (stripping) ratio is too high for opencast, underground methods become economic.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A rock retains 25% of its original parent isotope (half-life 0.7 Gyr). Its radiometric age is about:
questions.solution: 25% remaining ⇒ 2 half-lives ⇒ age = 2×0.7 = 1.4 Gyr.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The law of superposition reliably gives relative age only when the strata are:
questions.solution: Tectonic overturning can invert beds, so superposition needs an undisturbed (right-way-up) sequence.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A mineral specimen of mass 54 g has a volume of 30 cm³. Its specific gravity is about:
questions.solution: SG = mass/volume = 54/30 = 1.8.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The fastest seismic body waves, which arrive first at a station, are:
questions.solution: P-waves are compressional and travel fastest, arriving before slower S and surface waves.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Quartz (hardness 7) will scratch, but not be scratched by, which mineral?
questions.solution: A mineral scratches anything softer; quartz (7) scratches feldspar (6) but not topaz/corundum/diamond.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A mineral has passed through 1 half-lives of an isotope whose half-life is 1.3 (in the given time units). Its age is:
questions.solution: Age = number of half-lives × half-life = 1×1.3 = 1.3.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The most abundant single mineral group in the crust is:
questions.solution: Feldspars make up roughly half of the Earth's crust by volume.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: On the block diagram shown, the inclined bedding plane dips at 30°. The dip direction is, by definition:
options: Perpendicular to the strike, in the direction of steepest descent
questions.solution: Dip (here 30°) is measured perpendicular to strike, down the line of steepest slope of the plane.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 240 130" width="240" height="130"><polygon points="20,40 200,40 220,70 40,70" fill="#e2e8f0" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.5"/><line x1="40" y1="70" x2="220" y2="70" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="2"/><text x="120" y="86" font-size="9" fill="#2563eb">strike</text><line x1="120" y1="70" x2="160" y2="110" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="2"/><text x="150" y="100" font-size="10" fill="#dc2626">dip 30°</text><path d="M120 70 A 30 30 0 0 1 150 92" fill="none" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="1"/></svg>
figure.caption: Strike and dip of a bed

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Porosity of a rock refers to its:
questions.solution: Porosity is the percentage of void space; permeability is the ability to transmit fluid.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The theory that the Earth's lithosphere is divided into moving plates is:
questions.solution: Plate tectonics explains earthquakes, volcanism and mountain building by plate interactions.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A bed dipping at 30° shows a width of 20 m across a horizontal surface. Its true (perpendicular) thickness is about:
questions.solution: True thickness = width × sin(dip) = 20×sin30° ≈ 10 m.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The upper surface of the zone of saturation in the ground is the:
questions.solution: The water table marks the top of the saturated zone, below which pores are water-filled.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An aquifer (K = 10 m/day) of cross-section 500 m² has a hydraulic gradient of 0.01. The groundwater discharge is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Basalt is a(n):
questions.solution: Basalt is the fine-grained, dark, mafic rock formed from rapidly cooled lava.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The most abundant group of rock-forming minerals in the Earth's crust is the:
questions.solution: Silicate minerals (feldspar, quartz, mica, etc.) dominate the crust.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Using a simplified proxy where calorific value ≈ 70 × fixed-carbon%, a coal with 60% fixed carbon has a calorific value of about:
questions.solution: CV ≈ 70 × 60 = 4200 kcal/kg (illustrative proxy).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An upward-arching fold, with the oldest beds in its core, is called a(n):
questions.solution: An anticline arches upward; its limbs dip away from the axis and oldest rocks lie in the core.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An aquifer (K = 10 m/day) of cross-section 100 m² has a hydraulic gradient of 0.02. The groundwater discharge is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The principle of original horizontality states that sediments are originally deposited:
questions.solution: Tilted/folded strata indicate later deformation, since beds start horizontal.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The progressive transformation of plant matter into higher-rank coal is called:
questions.solution: Coalification increases carbon and reduces moisture/volatiles as burial heat and pressure rise.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An opencast block of 20000 m² has 30 m of overburden above the seam. The overburden volume to be removed is:
questions.solution: Volume = area × depth = 20000×30 = 600000 m³ = 0.6 million m³.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The colour of a mineral's powder, seen when it is rubbed on a porcelain plate, is its:
questions.solution: Streak is the colour of the fine powder, often more diagnostic than the surface colour.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: In the faulted block shown, the hanging wall has moved down relative to the footwall. This is a:
questions.solution: Hanging wall down = normal fault, caused by extension/tension.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 260 130" width="260" height="130"><line x1="40" y1="25" x2="150" y2="80" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="2"/><rect x="20" y="40" width="120" height="70" fill="#fde68a" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1" transform="skewX(-10)"/><rect x="150" y="70" width="100" height="70" fill="#bfdbfe" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1"/><line x1="60" y1="55" x2="135" y2="92" stroke="#1e293b" stroke-width="1.5" stroke-dasharray="2 2"/><line x1="160" y1="90" x2="245" y2="90" stroke="#1e293b" stroke-width="1.5" stroke-dasharray="2 2"/><text x="190" y="82" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">hanging wall</text><text x="40" y="35" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">footwall</text></svg>
figure.caption: normal fault

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A porphyritic texture (large crystals in a fine groundmass) indicates:
options: Two stages of cooling — slow then fast
questions.solution: Early slow cooling grows phenocrysts; later rapid cooling forms the fine matrix.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Granite is best classified as a(n):
questions.solution: Granite cools slowly at depth, producing large crystals; it is felsic and intrusive.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A 4 m seam over 6 km² (density 1.4 t/m³) has a recovery factor of 0.6. The mineable reserve is about:
questions.solution: Mineable = area×thickness×density×recovery = 6M m²×4×1.4×0.6 ≈ 20.2 Mt.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Sandstone is a:
options: Clastic sedimentary rock made of sand-sized grains
questions.solution: Sandstone is a clastic rock of cemented sand (mostly quartz) grains.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A coal seam covers 5 km², is 5 m thick, with density 1.3 t/m³. The in-situ reserve is about:
questions.solution: Reserve = area×thickness×density = 5×10⁶ m²×5 m×1.3 t/m³ ≈ 32.5 Mt.
